description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, test, and deploy, using the debian Docker image for all jobs. In the build stage add a build-package job that runs `apt update`, `apt -y upgrade`, `apt install -y curl`, then executes `curl -sL https://gitlab.com/hassbian/CI/raw/master/hassbian-scripts/build_and_test.sh | bash -s ${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}`. In the test stage add a test-package job that runs the same `apt update`, `apt -y upgrade`, `apt install -y curl`, executes the same build_and_test.sh script with `curl -sL https://gitlab.com/hassbian/CI/raw/master/hassbian-scripts/build_and_test.sh | bash -s ${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}`, and finally installs the built package using `apt -y install ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/package.deb`. In the deploy stage add a deploy-to-repo job that runs `apt update`, `apt -y upgrade`, `apt install -y curl git`, and runs `curl -sL https://gitlab.com/hassbian/CI/raw/master/hassbian-scripts/distribute.sh | bash -s ${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}`. No caches, artifacts, or conditional rules are defined.
